{"content": "     1|"""
     2|WhatsApp platform adapter.
     3|
     4|WhatsApp integration is more complex than Telegram/Discord because:
     5|- No official bot API for personal accounts
     6|- Business API requires Meta Business verification
     7|- Most solutions use web-based automation
     8|
     9|This adapter supports multiple backends:
    10|1. WhatsApp Business API (requires Meta verification)
    11|2. whatsapp-web.js (via Node.js subprocess) - for personal accounts
    12|3. Baileys (via Node.js subprocess) - alternative for personal accounts
    13|
    14|For simplicity, we'll implement a generic interface that can work
    15|with different backends via a bridge pattern.
    16|"""
    17|
    18|import asyncio
    19|import json
    20|import logging
    21|import os
    22|import platform
    23|import re
    24|import shutil
    25|import signal
    26|import subprocess
    27|
    28|_IS_WINDOWS = platform.system() == "Windows"
    29|from pathlib import Path
    30|from typing import Dict, Optional, Any
    31|
    32|from hermes_constants import get_hermes_dir
    33|
    34|logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    35|
    36|
    37|def _kill_port_process(port: int) -> None:
    38|    """Kill any process listening on the given TCP port."""
    39|    try:
    40|        if _IS_WINDOWS:
    41|            # Use netstat to find the PID bound to this port, then taskkill
    42|            result = subprocess.run(
    43|                ["netstat", "-ano", "-p", "TCP"],
    44|                capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5,
    45|            )
    46|            for line in result.stdout.splitlines():
    47|                parts = line.split()
    48|                if len(parts) >= 5 and parts[3] == "LISTENING":
    49|                    local_addr = parts[1]
    50|                    if local_addr.endswith(f":{port}"):
    51|                        try:
    52|                            subprocess.run(
    53|                                ["taskkill", "/PID", parts[4], "/F"],
    54|                                capture_output=True, timeout=5,
    55|                            )
    56|                        except subprocess.SubprocessError:
    57|                            pass
    58|        else:
    59|            # Try fuser first (Linux), fall back to lsof (macOS / WSL2)
    60|            killed = False
    61|            try:
    62|                result = subprocess.run(
    63|                    ["fuser", f"{port}/tcp"],
    64|                    capture_output=True, timeout=5,
    65|                )
    66|                if result.returncode == 0:
    67|                    subprocess.run(
    68|                        ["fuser", "-k", f"{port}/tcp"],
    69|                        capture_output=True, timeout=5,
    70|                    )
    71|                    killed = True
    72|            except FileNotFoundError:
    73|                pass  # fuser not installed
    74|
    75|            if not killed:
    76|                try:
    77|                    result = subprocess.run(
    78|                        ["lsof", "-ti", f":{port}"],
    79|                        capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5,
    80|                    )
    81|                    for pid_str in result.stdout.strip().splitlines():
    82|                        try:
    83|                            os.kill(int(pid_str), signal.SIGTERM)
    84|                        except (ValueError, ProcessLookupError, PermissionError):
    85|                            pass
    86|                except FileNotFoundError:
    87|                    pass  # lsof not installed either
    88|    except Exception:
    89|        pass
    90|
    91|
    92|def _kill_stale_bridge_by_pidfile(session_path: Path) -> None:
    93|    """Kill a bridge process recorded in a PID file from a previous run.
    94|
    95|    The bridge writes ``bridge.pid`` into the session directory when it
    96|    starts.  If the gateway crashed without a clean shutdown the old bridge
    97|    process becomes orphaned — this helper finds and kills it.
    98|    """
    99|    pid_file = session_path / "bridge.pid"
   100|    if not pid_file.exists():
   101|        return
   102|    try:
   103|        pid = int(pid_file.read_text().strip())
   104|    except (ValueError, OSError, TypeError):
   105|        try:
   106|            pid_file.unlink()
   107|        except OSError:
   108|            pass
   109|        return
   110|    # ``os.kill(pid, 0)`` is NOT a no-op on Windows (bpo-14484) — use the
   111|    # cross-platform existence check before sending a real signal.
   112|    from gateway.status import _pid_exists
   113|    if _pid_exists(pid):
   114|        try:
   115|            os.kill(pid, signal.SIGTERM)
   116|            logger.info("[whatsapp] Killed stale bridge PID %d from pidfile", pid)
   117|        except (ProcessLookupError, PermissionError, OSError):
   118|            pass
   119|    try:
   120|        pid_file.unlink()
   121|    except OSError:
   122|        pass
   123|
   124|
   125|def _write_bridge_pidfile(session_path: Path, pid: int) -> None:
   126|    """Write the bridge PID to a file for later cleanup."""
   127|    try:
   128|        (session_path / "bridge.pid").write_text(str(pid))
   129|    except OSError:
   130|        pass
   131|
   132|
   133|def _terminate_bridge_process(proc, *, force: bool = False) -> None:
   134|    """Terminate the bridge process using process-tree semantics where possible."""
   135|    if _IS_WINDOWS:
   136|        cmd = ["taskkill", "/PID", str(proc.pid), "/T"]
   137|        if force:
   138|            cmd.append("/F")
   139|        try:
   140|            result = subprocess.run(
   141|                cmd,
   142|                capture_output=True,
   143|                text=True,
   144|                timeout=10,
   145|            )
   146|        except FileNotFoundError:
   147|            if force:
   148|                proc.kill()
   149|            else:
   150|                proc.terminate()
   151|            return
   152|
   153|        if result.returncode != 0:
   154|            details = (result.stderr or result.stdout or "").strip()
   155|            raise OSError(details or f"taskkill failed for PID {proc.pid}")
   156|        return
   157|
   158|    import psutil
   159|    try:
   160|        parent = psutil.Process(proc.pid)
   161|        children = parent.children(recursive=True)
   162|        if force:
   163|            for child in children:
   164|                try:
   165|                    child.kill()
   166|                except psutil.NoSuchProcess:
   167|                    pass
   168|            parent.kill()
   169|        else:
   170|            for child in children:
   171|                try:
   172|                    child.terminate()
   173|                except psutil.NoSuchProcess:
   174|                    pass
   175|            parent.terminate()
   176|    except psutil.NoSuchProcess:
   177|        return
   178|
   179|import sys
   180|sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]))
   181|
   182|from gateway.config import Platform, PlatformConfig
   183|from gateway.platforms.base import (
   184|    BasePlatformAdapter,
   185|    MessageEvent,
   186|    MessageType,
   187|    SendResult,
   188|    SUPPORTED_DOCUMENT_TYPES,
   189|    cache_image_from_url,
   190|    cache_audio_from_url,
   191|)
   192|
   193|
   194|def check_whatsapp_requirements() -> bool:
   195|    """
   196|    Check if WhatsApp dependencies are available.
   197|    
   198|    WhatsApp requires a Node.js bridge for most implementations.
   199|    """
   200|    # Check for Node.js.  Resolve via shutil.which so we respect PATHEXT
   201|    # (node.exe vs node) and get a meaningful "not installed" signal
   202|    # instead of spawning a cmd flash on Windows.
   203|    _node = shutil.which("node")
   204|    if not _node:
   205|        return False
   206|    try:
   207|        result = subprocess.run(
   208|            [_node, "--version"],
   209|            capture_output=True,
   210|            text=True,
   211|            timeout=5
   212|        )
   213|        return result.returncode == 0
   214|    except Exception:
   215|        return False
   216|
   217|
   218|class WhatsAppAdapter(BasePlatformAdapter):
   219|    """
   220|    WhatsApp adapter.
   221|    
   222|    This implementation uses a simple HTTP bridge pattern where:
   223|    1. A Node.js process runs the WhatsApp Web client
   224|    2. Messages are forwarded via HTTP/IPC to this Python adapter
   225|    3. Responses are sent back through the bridge
   226|    
   227|    The actual Node.js bridge implementation can vary:
   228|    - whatsapp-web.js based
   229|    - Baileys based
   230|    - Business API based
   231|    
   232|    Configuration:
   233|    - bridge_script: Path to the Node.js bridge script
   234|    - bridge_port: Port for HTTP communication (default: 3000)
   235|    - session_path: Path to store WhatsApp session data
   236|    - dm_policy: "open" | "allowlist" | "disabled" — how DMs are handled (default: "open")
   237|    - allow_from: List of sender IDs allowed in DMs (when dm_policy="allowlist")
   238|    - group_policy: "open" | "allowlist" | "disabled" — which groups are processed (default: "open")
   239|    - group_allow_from: List of group JIDs allowed (when group_policy="allowlist")
   240|    """
   241|    
   242|    # WhatsApp message limits — practical UX limit, not protocol max.
   243|    # WhatsApp allows ~65K but long messages are unreadable on mobile.
   244|    MAX_MESSAGE_LENGTH = 4096
   245|    DEFAULT_REPLY_PREFIX = "⚕ *Hermes Agent*\n────────────\n"
   246|    
   247|    # Default bridge location relative to the hermes-agent install
   248|    _DEFAULT_BRIDGE_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2] / "scripts" / "whatsapp-bridge"
   249|
   250|    def __init__(self, config: PlatformConfig):
   251|        super().__init__(config, Platform.WHATSAPP)
   252|        self._bridge_process: Optional[subprocess.Popen] = None
   253|        self._bridge_port: int = config.extra.get("bridge_port", 3000)
   254|        self._bridge_script: Optional[str] = config.extra.get(
   255|            "bridge_script",
   256|            str(self._DEFAULT_BRIDGE_DIR / "bridge.js"),
   257|        )
   258|        self._session_path: Path = Path(config.extra.get(
   259|            "session_path",
   260|            get_hermes_dir("platforms/whatsapp/session", "whatsapp/session")
   261|        ))
   262|        self._reply_prefix: Optional[str] = config.extra.get("reply_prefix")
   263|        self._dm_policy = str(config.extra.get("dm_policy") or os.getenv("WHATSAPP_DM_POLICY", "open")).strip().lower()
   264|        self._allow_from = self._coerce_allow_list(config.extra.get("allow_from") or config.extra.get("allowFrom"))
   265|        self._group_policy = str(config.extra.get("group_policy") or os.getenv("WHATSAPP_GROUP_POLICY", "open")).strip().lower()
   266|        self._group_allow_from = self._coerce_allow_list(config.extra.get("group_allow_from") or config.extra.get("groupAllowFrom"))
   267|        self._mention_patterns = self._compile_mention_patterns()
   268|        self._message_queue: asyncio.Queue = asyncio.Queue()
   269|        self._bridge_log_fh = None
   270|        self._bridge_log: Optional[Path] = None
   271|        self._poll_task: Optional[asyncio.Task] = None
   272|        self._http_session: Optional["aiohttp.ClientSession"] = None
   273|        # Set to True by disconnect() before we SIGTERM our child bridge so
   274|        # _check_managed_bridge_exit() can distinguish an intentional
   275|        # shutdown-time exit (returncode -15 / -2 / 0) from a real crash.
   276|        # Without this, every graceful gateway shutdown/restart would log
   277|        # "Fatal whatsapp adapter error" plus dispatch a fatal-error
   278|        # notification before the normal "✓ whatsapp disconnected" fires.
   279|        self._shutting_down: bool = False
   280|
   281|    def _effective_reply_prefix(self) -> str:
   282|        """Return the prefix the Node bridge will add in self-chat mode."""
   283|        whatsapp_mode = os.getenv("WHATSAPP_MODE", "self-chat")
   284|        if whatsapp_mode != "self-chat":
   285|            return ""
   286|        if self._reply_prefix is not None:
   287|            return self._reply_prefix.replace("\\n", "\n")
   288|        env_prefix = os.getenv("WHATSAPP_REPLY_PREFIX")
   289|        if env_prefix is not None:
   290|            return env_prefix.replace("\\n", "\n")
   291|        return self.DEFAULT_REPLY_PREFIX
   292|
   293|    def _outgoing_chunk_limit(self) -> int:
   294|        """Reserve room for the bridge-side prefix so final WhatsApp text fits."""
   295|        prefix_len = len(self._effective_reply_prefix())
   296|        # Keep enough space for truncate_message's pagination indicator and
   297|        # code-fence repair even if a user configures a very long prefix.
   298|        return max(1024, self.MAX_MESSAGE_LENGTH - prefix_len)
   299|
   300|    def _whatsapp_require_mention(self) -> bool:
   301|        configured = self.config.extra.get("require_mention")
   302|        if configured is not None:
   303|            if isinstance(configured, str):
   304|                return configured.lower() in {"true", "1", "yes", "on"}
   305|            return bool(configured)
   306|        return os.getenv("WHATSAPP_REQUIRE_MENTION", "false").lower() in {"true", "1", "yes", "on"}
   307|
   308|    def _whatsapp_free_response_chats(self) -> set[str]:
   309|        raw = self.config.extra.get("free_response_chats")
   310|        if raw is None:
   311|            raw = os.getenv("WHATSAPP_FREE_RESPONSE_CHATS", "")
   312|        if isinstance(raw, list):
   313|            return {str(part).strip() for part in raw if str(part).strip()}
   314|        return {part.strip() for part in str(raw).split(",") if part.strip()}
   315|
   316|    @staticmethod
   317|    def _coerce_allow_list(raw) -> set[str]:
   318|        """Parse allow_from / group_allow_from from config or env var."""
   319|        if raw is None:
   320|            return set()
   321|        if isinstance(raw, list):
   322|            return {str(part).strip() for part in raw if str(part).strip()}
   323|        return {part.strip() for part in str(raw).split(",") if part.strip()}
   324|
   325|    @staticmethod
   326|    def _is_broadcast_chat(chat_id: str) -> bool:
   327|        """True for WhatsApp pseudo-chats that aren't real conversations.
   328|
   329|        Covers Status updates (Stories) and Channel/Newsletter broadcasts.
   330|        These show up as inbound messages on Baileys but the agent should
   331|        never reply — answering a Story update spams the contact's status
   332|        feed, and Channel posts aren't addressable in the first place.
   333|        """
   334|        if not chat_id:
   335|            return False
   336|        cid = chat_id.strip().lower()
   337|        if cid == "status@broadcast":
   338|            return True
   339|        # @broadcast suffix covers status@broadcast plus any future
   340|        # broadcast-list variants. @newsletter is the Channel JID suffix.
   341|        if cid.endswith("@broadcast") or cid.endswith("@newsletter"):
   342|            return True
   343|        return False
   344|
   345|    def _is_dm_allowed(self, sender_id: str) -> bool:
   346|        """Check whether a DM from the given sender should be processed."""
   347|        if self._dm_policy == "disabled":
   348|            return False
   349|        if self._dm_policy == "allowlist":
   350|            return sender_id in self._allow_from
   351|        # "open" — all DMs allowed
   352|        return True
   353|
   354|    def _is_group_allowed(self, chat_id: str) -> bool:
   355|        """Check whether a group chat should be processed."""
   356|        if self._group_policy == "disabled":
   357|            return False
   358|        if self._group_policy == "allowlist":
   359|            return chat_id in self._group_allow_from
   360|        # "open" — all groups allowed
   361|        return True
   362|
   363|    def _compile_mention_patterns(self):
   364|        patterns = self.config.extra.get("mention_patterns")
   365|        if patterns is None:
   366|            raw = os.getenv("WHATSAPP_MENTION_PATTERNS", "").strip()
   367|            if raw:
   368|                try:
   369|                    patterns = json.loads(raw)
   370|                except Exception:
   371|                    patterns = [part.strip() for part in raw.splitlines() if part.strip()]
   372|                    if not patterns:
   373|                        patterns = [part.strip() for part in raw.split(",") if part.strip()]
   374|        if patterns is None:
   375|            return []
   376|        if isinstance(patterns, str):
   377|            patterns = [patterns]
   378|        if not isinstance(patterns, list):
   379|            logger.warning("[%s] whatsapp mention_patterns must be a list or string; got %s", self.name, type(patterns).__name__)
   380|            return []
   381|
   382|        compiled = []
   383|        for pattern in patterns:
   384|            if not isinstance(pattern, str) or not pattern.strip():
   385|                continue
   386|            try:
   387|                compiled.append(re.compile(pattern, re.IGNORECASE))
   388|            except re.error as exc:
   389|                logger.warning("[%s] Invalid WhatsApp mention pattern %r: %s", self.name, pattern, exc)
   390|        if compiled:
   391|            logger.info("[%s] Loaded %d WhatsApp mention pattern(s)", self.name, len(compiled))
   392|        return compiled
   393|
   394|    @staticmethod
   395|    def _normalize_whatsapp_id(value: Optional[str]) -> str:
   396|        if not value:
   397|            return ""
   398|        normalized = str(value).strip()
   399|        if ":" in normalized and "@" in normalized:
   400|            normalized = normalized.replace(":", "@", 1)
   401|        return normalized
   402|
   403|    def _bot_ids_from_message(self, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> set[str]:
   404|        bot_ids = set()
   405|        for candidate in data.get("botIds") or []:
   406|            normalized = self._normalize_whatsapp_id(candidate)
   407|            if normalized:
   408|                bot_ids.add(normalized)
   409|        return bot_ids
   410|
   411|    def _message_is_reply_to_bot(self, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
   412|        quoted_participant = self._normalize_whatsapp_id(data.get("quotedParticipant"))
   413|        if not quoted_participant:
   414|            return False
   415|        return quoted_participant in self._bot_ids_from_message(data)
   416|
   417|    def _message_mentions_bot(self, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
   418|        bot_ids = self._bot_ids_from_message(data)
   419|        if not bot_ids:
   420|            return False
   421|        mentioned_ids = {
   422|            nid
   423|            for candidate in (data.get("mentionedIds") or [])
   424|            if (nid := self._normalize_whatsapp_id(candidate))
   425|        }
   426|        if mentioned_ids & bot_ids:
   427|            return True
   428|
   429|        body = str(data.get("body") or "")
   430|        lower_body = body.lower()
   431|        for bot_id in bot_ids:
   432|            bare_id = bot_id.split("@", 1)[0].lower()
   433|            if bare_id and (f"@{bare_id}" in lower_body or bare_id in lower_body):
   434|                return True
   435|        return False
   436|
   437|    def _message_matches_mention_patterns(self, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
   438|        if not self._mention_patterns:
   439|            return False
   440|        body = str(data.get("body") or "")
   441|        return any(pattern.search(body) for pattern in self._mention_patterns)
   442|
   443|    def _clean_bot_mention_text(self, text: str, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:
   444|        if not text:
   445|            return text
   446|        bot_ids = self._bot_ids_from_message(data)
   447|        cleaned = text
   448|        for bot_id in bot_ids:
   449|            bare_id = bot_id.split("@", 1)[0]
   450|            if bare_id:
   451|                cleaned = re.sub(rf"@{re.escape(bare_id)}\b[,:\-]*\s*", "", cleaned)
   452|        return cleaned.strip() or text
   453|
   454|    def _should_process_message(self, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
   455|        chat_id_raw = str(data.get("chatId") or "")
   456|        # WhatsApp uses pseudo-chats for Status updates (Stories) and
   457|        # Channel/Newsletter broadcasts. These are not real conversations
   458|        # and the agent should never reply to them — even in self-chat mode
   459|        # where the bridge may surface them as "fromMe" events.
   460|        if self._is_broadcast_chat(chat_id_raw):
   461|            return False
   462|        is_group = data.get("isGroup", False)
   463|        if is_group:
   464|            chat_id = chat_id_raw
   465|            if not self._is_group_allowed(chat_id):
   466|                return False
   467|        else:
   468|            sender_id = str(data.get("senderId") or data.get("from") or "")
   469|            if not self._is_dm_allowed(sender_id):
   470|                return False
   471|            # DMs that pass the policy gate are always processed
   472|            return True
   473|        # Group messages: check mention / free-response settings
   474|        chat_id = str(data.get("chatId") or "")
   475|        if chat_id in self._whatsapp_free_response_chats():
   476|            return True
   477|        if not self._whatsapp_require_mention():
   478|            return True
   479|        body = str(data.get("body") or "").strip()
   480|        if body.startswith("/"):
   481|            return True
   482|        if self._message_is_reply_to_bot(data):
   483|            return True
   484|        if self._message_mentions_bot(data):
   485|            return True
   486|        return self._message_matches_mention_patterns(data)
   487|    
   488|    async def connect(self) -> bool:
   489|        """
   490|        Start the WhatsApp bridge.
   491|        
   492|        This launches the Node.js bridge process and waits for it to be ready.
   493|        """
   494|        if not check_whatsapp_requirements():

   498|                "Node.js is not installed — install Node.js and re-run `hermes gateway`.",
   499|                retryable=False,

messages.reasoning:
Now I see the issue. The bridge.log shows multiple "WhatsApp bridge listening on port 3000" messages and multiple EADDRINUSE errors. This means:

1. There are MULTIPLE bridge processes that have been started over time
2. They keep crashing with EADDRINUSE because something else is already on port 3000
3. But the current process (PID 3616576) somehow "succeeded" in connecting to WhatsApp... but wait, the curl returns Next.js HTML, not the bridge.

Wait, the bridge.log shows the CURRENT process (PID 3616576) at the end: `{"time":[REDACTED_PHONE],"pid":3616576...}`. But if it's listening on port 3000, why do we get Next.js?

OH WAIT. The bridge might be bound to port 3000 but maybe there's a Traefik or nginx reverse proxy that's intercepting? No, curl to 127.0.0.1:3000 should bypass any reverse proxy.

Unless... there is ANOTHER node process also on port 3000 that started AFTER the bridge but the ss output only shows one. Let me check if maybe the bridge is actually running on IPv6 only or something, and the Next.js app is on IPv4. But ss shows `0.0.0.0:3000` with the bridge PID.

Actually, I think I see the issue now. The bridge log shows "listening on port 3000" but the bridge might have crashed and then a systemd service or something else started a Next.js app on port 3000. But ss shows PID 3616576 on port 3000.

Wait... maybe `ss` is lying or caching? Or maybe the bridge process was REPLACED but the PID file wasn't updated? Let me check the bridge.pid again — it says 3616576, which matches the running process.

But if I run `curl 127.0.0.1:3000` and get Next.js, and `ss` says only PID 3616576 is on port 3000... then PID 3616576 MUST be serving Next.js. But its cmdline says it's running bridge.js.

UNLESS... the bridge.js file itself has been replaced or corrupted to serve a Next.js app? That seems impossible. But let me check the bridge.js file to make sure it's the real bridge.

Actually, I just realized something. Maybe the node process DID start as the bridge, but then it `require()`'d something that started a Next.js server, or maybe there's a package.json with a main entry that's wrong. Or maybe the bridge.js has been modified to be a Next.js app.

Let me check the bridge.js file to see what it actually contains. I already read the first 500 lines and it looked correct (Baileys, Express, etc.). But let me verify the file hasn't been tampered with.

Actually, let me look more carefully at the `ss` output. It shows:
```
LISTEN 0 511 0.0.0.0:3000 0.0.0.0:* users:(("node",pid=3616576,fd=21))
```

And the nc output shows the response is indeed coming from whatever is listening on that socket. 

Wait, I have a theory. What if the bridge.js `import` statements are resolving to completely different modules because of some symlinks or npm workspace issues? The cwd is `/root/.hermes/hermes-agent`, not the bridge directory. Node module resolution might be loading modules from `/root/.hermes/hermes-agent/node_modules` instead of `/root/.hermes/hermes-agent/scripts/whatsapp-bridge/node_modules`.

And if there's an Express app or Next.js app in the hermes-agent root's node_modules... no, that still wouldn't make bridge.js serve Next.js content.

Actually, let me check if maybe the bridge crashed and systemd/Docker restarted something else. But the process cmdline clearly shows bridge.js.

Let me take a different approach. Let me kill the bridge process, verify port 3000 is free, then start the bridge manually in the foreground to see what happens. Also check if there's a Next.js app that was supposed to be on a different port.

Actually, wait. Let me check `docker ps` — maybe there's a Docker container mapping port 3000, and `ss` inside the container namespace shows the container's PID which happens to match the host PID? No, that doesn't make sense because `ss` shows host PIDs.

Hmm, let me just kill the process, check port 3000, and restart the bridge manually.

messages.reasoning:
Now I see the full picture:

1. The gateway IS running as a systemd user service (PID 3519481)
2. The gateway has been trying to connect to WhatsApp on port 3000
3. But port 3000 is DNAT'd to a Docker container (172.27.0.2:3000), which serves the Next.js "De Abasto a Casa" app
4. So the gateway gets 404 HTML from the Next.js app instead of the bridge
5. The cron jobs are failing with "WhatsApp bridge error (404)"

The bridge process itself (when running on port 3000) couldn't actually receive traffic because of the iptables DNAT rule. And the bridge kept crashing with EADDRINUSE because multiple instances were competing for port 3000.

Now I have the bridge running on port 3007, and it's responding correctly. But I need to:
1. Update the config to set the WhatsApp bridge port to 3007
2. Make sure the gateway knows about WhatsApp
3. Restart the gateway so it picks up the new config and connects to the bridge on port 3007

Also, looking at the gateway logs, WhatsApp is being used by cron jobs — so it's at least partially configured. But the gateway.platforms section doesn't have an explicit `whatsapp` entry. The gateway might be auto-enabling it from the top-level `whatsapp:` config, or maybe there's another config file.

Let me update the config to add the bridge_port and make sure WhatsApp is enabled. Then restart the gateway.

Actually, looking more carefully at the config, I see `whatsapp: {}` at the top level (line 447). I should update that to include `bridge_port: 3007` and `mode: bot`. And I should add a `whatsapp` platform under `gateway.platforms` with `enabled: true`.

Wait, but the cron jobs are already sending to WhatsApp, which means the gateway DOES have WhatsApp configured somehow. Maybe it's through a different config or the `gateway.platforms.whatsapp` was omitted from the grep output earlier? Let me re-check.

Actually no — the cron jobs use the `send_message` tool which might be trying to send via WhatsApp directly through the bridge, not through the gateway platform adapter. Or maybe the gateway adapter is instantiated from the top-level `whatsapp:` config.

Regardless, I need to:
1. Update `whatsapp:` in config.yaml to set `bridge_port: 3007`
2. Restart the gateway to pick up the change

Let me patch the config.

     2|Gateway configuration management.
     3|
     4|Handles loading and validating configuration for:
     5|- Connected platforms (Telegram, Discord, WhatsApp, Weixin, and more)
     6|- Home channels for each platform
     7|- Session reset policies
     8|- Delivery preferences
     9|"""
    10|
    11|import logging
    12|import os
    13|import json
    14|from pathlib import Path
    15|from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    16|from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Any, Callable

    20|from utils import is_truthy_value
    21|
    22|logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    23|
    24|
    25|def _coerce_bool(value: Any, default: bool = True) -> bool:
    26|    """Coerce bool-ish config values, preserving a caller-provided default."""
    27|    if value is None:
    28|        return default
    29|    if isinstance(value, str):
    30|        lowered = value.strip().lower()
    31|        if lowered in {"true", "1", "yes", "on"}:
    32|            return True
    33|        if lowered in {"false", "0", "no", "off"}:
    34|            return False
    35|        return default
    36|    return is_truthy_value(value, default=default)
    37|
    38|
    39|def _coerce_float(value: Any, default: float) -> float:
    40|    """Coerce numeric config values, falling back on malformed input."""
    41|    if value is None:
    42|        return default
    43|    try:
    44|        return float(value)
    45|    except (TypeError, ValueError):
    46|        return default
    47|
    48|
    49|def _coerce_int(value: Any, default: int) -> int:
    50|    """Coerce integer config values, falling back on malformed input."""
    51|    if value is None:
    52|        return default
    53|    try:
    54|        return int(value)
    55|    except (TypeError, ValueError):
    56|        return default
    57|
    58|
    59|def _normalize_unauthorized_dm_behavior(value: Any, default: str = "pair") -> str:
    60|    """Normalize unauthorized DM behavior to a supported value."""
    61|    if isinstance(value, str):
    62|        normalized = value.strip().lower()
    63|        if normalized in {"pair", "ignore"}:
    64|            return normalized
    65|    return default
    66|
    67|
    68|def _normalize_notice_delivery(value: Any, default: str = "public") -> str:
    69|    """Normalize notice delivery mode to a supported value."""
    70|    if isinstance(value, str):
    71|        normalized = value.strip().lower()
    72|        if normalized in {"public", "private"}:
    73|            return normalized
    74|    return default

    77|def _ensure_platform_extra_dict(platforms_data: dict, name: str) -> tuple[dict, dict]:
    78|    """Get-or-create ``platforms_data[name]`` and its nested ``extra`` dict.
    79|
    80|    Both slots are coerced to ``{}`` if a non-dict value is encountered, so
    81|    callers can safely write keys without type-checking.  Returns
    82|    ``(plat_data, extra)`` for in-place mutation.
    83|    """
    84|    plat_data = platforms_data.setdefault(name, {})
    85|    if not isinstance(plat_data, dict):
    86|        plat_data = {}
    87|        platforms_data[name] = plat_data
    88|    extra = plat_data.setdefault("extra", {})
    89|    if not isinstance(extra, dict):
    90|        extra = {}
    91|        plat_data["extra"] = extra

    94|
    95|# Module-level cache for bundled platform plugin names (lives outside the
    96|# enum so it doesn't become an accidental enum member).
    97|_Platform__bundled_plugin_names: Optional[set] = None
    98|
    99|
   100|class Platform(Enum):
   101|    """Supported messaging platforms.
   102|
   103|    Built-in platforms have explicit members.  Plugin platforms use dynamic
   104|    members created on-demand by ``_missing_()`` so that
   105|    ``Platform("irc")`` works without modifying this enum.  Dynamic members
   106|    are cached in ``_value2member_map_`` for identity-stable comparisons.
   107|    """
   108|    LOCAL = "local"
   109|    TELEGRAM = "telegram"
   110|    DISCORD = "discord"
   111|    WHATSAPP = "whatsapp"
   112|    SLACK = "slack"
   113|    SIGNAL = "signal"
   114|    MATTERMOST = "mattermost"
   115|    MATRIX = "matrix"
   116|    HOMEASSISTANT = "homeassistant"
   117|    EMAIL = "email"
   118|    SMS = "sms"
   119|    DINGTALK = "dingtalk"
   120|    API_SERVER = "api_server"
   121|    WEBHOOK = "webhook"
   122|    MSGRAPH_WEBHOOK = "msgraph_webhook"
   123|    FEISHU = "feishu"
   124|    WECOM = "wecom"
   125|    WECOM_CALLBACK = "wecom_callback"
   126|    WEIXIN = "weixin"
   127|    BLUEBUBBLES = "bluebubbles"
   128|    QQBOT = "qqbot"
   129|    YUANBAO = "yuanbao"
   130|    @classmethod
   131|    def _missing_(cls, value):
   132|        """Accept unknown platform names only for known plugin adapters.
   133|
   134|        Creates a pseudo-member cached in ``_value2member_map_`` so that
   135|        ``Platform("irc") is Platform("irc")`` holds True (identity-stable).
   136|        Arbitrary strings are rejected to prevent enum pollution.
   137|        """
   138|        if not isinstance(value, str) or not value.strip():
   139|            return None
   140|        # Normalise to lowercase to avoid case mismatches in config
   141|        value = value.strip().lower()
   142|        # Check cache first (another call may have created it already)
   143|        if value in cls._value2member_map_:
   144|            return cls._value2member_map_[value]
   145|
   146|        # Only create pseudo-members for bundled plugin platforms (discovered
   147|        # via filesystem scan) or runtime-registered plugin platforms.
   148|        global _Platform__bundled_plugin_names
   149|        if _Platform__bundled_plugin_names is None:
   150|            _Platform__bundled_plugin_names = cls._scan_bundled_plugin_platforms()
   151|        if value in _Platform__bundled_plugin_names:
   152|            pseudo = object.__new__(cls)
   153|            pseudo._value_ = value
   154|            pseudo._name_ = value.upper().replace("-", "_").replace(" ", "_")
   155|            cls._value2member_map_[value] = pseudo
   156|            cls._member_map_[pseudo._name_] = pseudo
   157|            return pseudo
   158|
   159|        # Runtime-registered plugins (e.g. user-installed, discovered after
   160|        # the enum was defined).
   161|        try:
   162|            from gateway.platform_registry import platform_registry
   163|            if platform_registry.is_registered(value):
   164|                pseudo = object.__new__(cls)
   165|                pseudo._value_ = value
   166|                pseudo._name_ = value.upper().replace("-", "_").replace(" ", "_")
   167|                cls._value2member_map_[value] = pseudo
   168|                cls._member_map_[pseudo._name_] = pseudo
   169|                return pseudo
   170|        except Exception:
   171|            pass
   172|
   173|        return None
   174|
   175|    @classmethod
   176|    def _scan_bundled_plugin_platforms(cls) -> set:
   177|        """Return names of bundled platform plugins under ``plugins/platforms/``."""
   178|        names: set = set()
   179|        try:
   180|            platforms_dir = Path(__file__).parent.parent / "plugins" / "platforms"
   181|            if platforms_dir.is_dir():
   182|                for child in platforms_dir.iterdir():

   188|                            or (child / "plugin.yml").exists()
   189|                        )
   190|                    ):

   194|        return names

   201|
   202|@dataclass

   205|    Default destination for a platform.
   206|    
   207|    When a cron job specifies deliver="telegram" without a specific chat ID,
   208|    messages are sent to this home channel. Thread-aware platforms may also

   240|    Controls when sessions reset (lose context).

   242|    Modes:
   243|    - "daily": Reset at a specific hour each day
   244|    - "idle": Reset after N minutes of inactivity

   274|            idle_minutes=idle_minutes if idle_minutes is not None else 1440,
   275|            notify=_coerce_bool(notify, True),
